rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID ACHTERKIRCHEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT JESUS MONTIEL's Motion To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Motion granted. The issue of whether the subpoena was served without adequate notice is moot. The Court's March 2 Order commanding plaintiff to appear March 12 for a debtor's examination and produce documents responsive to the subpoena supersedes any issue with the subpoena, as the Court clearly took control of the timing of discovery with the March 2 order. As plaintiff has not complied with the March 2 Order, and the present motion seems to be without basis and designed to delay the proceedings, there appears to be a willful violation of the Court's March 2 Order by both the debtor and counsel. Sanctions are awarded against debtor's counsel in the amount of $7,615 payable to plaintiff's counsel within 10 days of notice of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NEIGHBORS FOR PRESERVATION AND PROGRESS (A CALIFOR et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO BOARD OF APPEALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 12, PETITIONER NEIGHBORS FOR PRESERVATION AND PROGRESS Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandamus. The court denies the petition for writ of administrative mandamus. The court reviews the record as a whole and substantial evidence supports respondent's decision. See Carmel-By-The-Sea v. Bd. of Supervisors (1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 84. Respondent's decision of February 26, 2014, complies with Topanga Assn. for a Scenic Comm. v. County of LA (1974) 11 Cal.3d 506, 515, and there is a sufficient analytical bridge between the raw data and decision. Petitioner contends that respondent improperly relied on the Housing Accountability Act (Cal. Gov't Code sec. 65589.5 or "HAA") to issue its final decision. Respondent, however, does not cite the HAA and the decision is based on analyzing the San Francisco Planning Code and General Plan. Respondent originally believed that the project should be reduced to 4 floors, but upon further reflection found that 5 floors was feasible with further conditions. That final determination was not an abuse of discretion and substantial evidence supports the decision. It is true that some Commissioner's were uncertain whether the HAA applied, but petitioner fails to show that respondent's decision was actually based on a misapplication of the law. The court rejects petitioner's remaining arguments. Petitioner received due process and appeared at several hearings. Supervisor Weiner did not illegally taint the deliberative process as he was merely expressing his opinion under the First Amendment. See Eller Outdoor Advertising v. Bd. of Supervisors (1979) 89 Cal.App.3d 76. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories General By Defendant William Hedden And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant William Hedden And His Attorney Steven F. Helfand Jointly And Severly; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement; Declaration Of Rey Hassan And Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ROY MILLER, MARIO GALANDE, MILLER - GALANDE ANTIQUES Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories General By Defendant William Hedden And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant William Hedden And His Attorney Steven F. Helfand Jointly And Severally. Plaintiff's motions to compel are denied. Notice of motions to compel further responses to interrogatories must be filed and served within 45 days of receipt of responses. CCP § 2030.300(c). These three motions were not served until four days after that 45 day jurisdictional limit expired. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $4,250 payable within 14 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Re Motion To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN Motion To Set Aside Default. Granted. The default and default judgment are set aside. The Court finds that the only way to ensure that Robert Kwok Chu Chan is not adversely affected by the judgment in this action is to set aside the default and default judgment, particularly in light of the fact that Robert Kwok Chu Chan's home address is listed on the October 22, 2013 Entry of Default and the December 12, 2013 Default Judgment. rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Exactech, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 25, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KENDRA TANACEA'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Exactech, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions. (part 2 of 2) RPD #7 Defendant Exactech Inc. shall produce the documents requested, and provide a privilege log for each document withheld, identifying the document by date, Bates no., type of document, length in pages, preparer, recipient, general subject matter, and reason for withholding. RPD #9 Defendant Exactech Inc. shall produce the documents requested, redacted for 3rd party patient names or other identifying information. RPD # 10 and 11: Defendant Exactech Inc. shall produce the documents requested. The Court finds plaintiff has the right to conduct discovery even though the demurrer is pending. See Cal. Prac. Guide Civ. Pro. Before Trial Ch. 8B at sec. [8:59] Discovery after demurrer sustained: Deficiencies in the pleadings generally do not affect either party's right to conduct discovery. Thus, for example, discovery may continue after a demurrer has been sustained with leave to amend, although no amended complaint has yet been filed-i.e., no valid complaint need be on file. [Budget Finance Plan v. Sup.Ct. (McDowell) (1973) 34 CA3d 794, 797, 110 CR 302, 304] Sanctions granted in the amount of $7,300 against Exactech, Inc. and its counsel Thomas Frieder and Hassard Bonnington LLP, payable to plaintiff's counsel within 10 days of service of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Exactech, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 25, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KENDRA TANACEA'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Exactech, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Special Interrogatories, Set One, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Motion granted. Defendant Exactech shall respond to the following special interrogatories: Interrogatory Nos. 1, 2: Objections overruled. Defendant Exactech Inc. shall provide a complete response specifically identifying with particularity Exactech products that Dr. Tavernetti participated in designing and when. Nos. 3, 4: Objections overruled. Defendant Exactech Inc. shall provide a complete response. No. 5: Objections overruled. Defendant Exactech Inc. shall provide a complete response as to all consulting agreements with Dr. Tavernetti. No. 6, 7, 8, 10, 11, 12,13, 16, 17,18, and 19: Objections overruled. Defendant Exactech Inc. shall provide a complete response. The Court finds that Interrogatories No. 14, 15 are overbroad and compound, and are more properly the subject of a deposition. Sanctions granted in the amount of $6,000 against Exactech, Inc. and its counsel Thomas Frieder and Hassard Bonnington LLP, payable to plaintiff's counsel within 10 days of service of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: KENDRA L. TANACEA VS. RICHARD R. TAVERNETTI MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Exactech, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 25, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF KENDRA TANACEA'S Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Exactech, Inc. To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And For Order Striking Objections; Request For Sanctions. (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Motion granted. RPD #1: Defendant Exactech Inc. shall produce Exhibit A if it exists, and shall make a diligent effort to locate Exact A. RPD #2: Defendant Exactech Inc. shall produce the documents requested. The Court finds that the extension of time to 3/19 for "any agreed item above" (Exh. I Inijian Decl.) is sufficiently ambiguous that plaintiff's counsel had a right to rely on March 19 as her motion filing deadline for all discovery at issue. RPD #3, 4, 5, 6, 8: Defendant Exactech Inc. shall produce the documents requested. The Court finds that after balancing financial privacy interests against the plaintiff's need to conduct discovery, plaintiff should obtain the discovery because of the financial and business relationship already demonstrated between the defendants. (see part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Katie Hom'S Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Pure Entertainment, Llc'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 25, 2015, Line 3, Katie Hom's Motion To Compel Cross-Defendant Pure Entertainment, LLC'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Motion granted. The Court finds that the information sought by the interrogatories is discoverable. Further responses shall be served within 10 days of service of this order. Sanctions denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WAN LING SONG VS. TOMMY ZHONG YANG FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: To Compel Defendant To Execute Authorization For Release Of Cell Phone Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday March 25, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF WAN SONG'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Execute Authorization For Release Of Cell Phone Records. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Denied. Court has no power to compel a party to sign a release of cell phone records. Court notes that an issue (not before the Court on this motion) remains whether cell phone records are within "control" of a party in that he could readily obtain copies of said records from his cell phone provider in response to a demand for production of documents under CCP sec. 2031.010 et seq. rulings.Case Title: NATHALIE HESNARD VS. LYCEE FRANCAIS DE SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Lycee Francais De San Francisco'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT LYCEE FRANCAIS DE SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Sanctions. (Duplicate Entry - For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only) (Part 2 of 2.) The Court finds that Form Interrogatories 50.0 series do not apply in this action and plaintiff does not have to answer those interrogatories. Form Interrogatories, Employment: In this employment case, plaintiff must answer the following interrogatories, which are generally considered objection-proof as to form: 200.1-200.5; 202.1 (need a yes or no response; must break out response by each claim of discrimination , and must identify documents upon which each claim is based); 204.3 and 204.6 (more detail re dates and documents); 217.1 (must identify documents, if any) Plaintiff is not required to respond further to 204.1, 204.7 (this is directed to the employer, not the employee) Plaintiff is not required to respond further to special interrogatories 23 (compound and invades privacy under Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844 by seeking information about all medical conditions, not just those that have been tendered in this action.) or 24 (answer is sufficient). Plaintiff shall produce documents responsive to the following demands for production of documents (identifying to which numbered demand the documents are responsive) and, if applicable, a privilege log for documents withheld, within 14 days of service of this Order: 1-9; 20, 31, 42, 53, 10, 21, 23, 24, 25, 26, 29, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, 11 (provide privilege log where necessary. Communications about this claim with ordinary 3rd parties would not be protected from discovery), 22, 30 (privilege log if necessary) Plaintiff is not required to respond further to RPD No. 27 (overbroad and invades privacy under Britt v. Superior Court (1978) 20 Cal.3d 844 by seeking information about all medical conditions, not just those that have been tendered in this action.). Sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL RODRIGUEZ ET AL VS. SAW ENTERTAINMENT LTD, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT SAW ENTERTAINMENT LTD., Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Action. Motion to Compel Arbitration is granted. Under CCP § 1281.2, a court may deny a motion to compel arbitration when a party to the arbitration agreement is also a party to a pending court action with a third party that arises out of the same transaction or series of related transactions and presents a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. Henry v. Alcove Inv., Inc., (1991) 233 Cal. App. 3d 94. Plaintiffs have not established that Defendant has waived his right to compel arbitration. Further Plaintiffs have not shown a possibility of conflicting rulings on a common issue of law or fact. The arbitration agreement explicitly includes any and all claims arising from the employment relationship. Plaintiff's claims arise under the employment relationship, therefore are within the scope of the arbitration agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DIGITAL REALTY TRUST, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION ET AL VS. MICHAEL F. FOUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Michael F. Foust To Compel Further Responses To His First Set Of Requests For Production To Plaintiffs Digital Realty Trust, Inc., Digital Realty Trust, L.P., And Dlr, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, March 25, 2015, Line 7, DEFENDANT MICHAEL FOUST's Motion To Compel Further Responses To His First Set Of Requests For Production To Plaintiffs Digital Realty Trust, Inc., Digital Realty Trust, L.P., And DLR, LLC. Pro Tem Judge Charles Geerhart, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following: Hearing required. The parties should be prepared to discuss: 1) can some of the most broad document demands be narrowed either in scope or geographic region involved ? See, e.g., RPD 16, 17, 18. 2) Since DLR has an obligation under CCP 2031.010 et seq. to perform a diligent search of all responsive writings in its possession, custody or control, for all documents requested, why does it matter who is designated as a custodian? 3) with DLR's claim for intentional interference with prospective economic advantage dropped, and the limiting of the 2019.210 trade secret identification, why should writings concerning prospective business activities of DLR be discoverable? Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to chuck@paoligeerhart.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
